qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: XML injection via ProcessingInstruction and Comment data mutation bypasses serialization boundary
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Same vulnerable function (createProcessingInstruction), same missing validation of '?>' in PI data, and same serializer code that emits PI data verbatim without escaping. The finding identifies the identical root cause described in the advisory: createProcessingInstruction stores data without checking for '?>' (lib/dom.js:2266), and the serializer concatenates '<?', target, space, node.data, and '?>' without splitting or escaping '?>' within data (lib/dom.js:3064).

selected_finding.title: XML injection via ProcessingInstruction and Comment data mutation bypasses serialization boundary

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The finding identifies the same vulnerable code and root cause as the advisory: createProcessingInstruction does not validate data for '?>' and the serializer emits PI data verbatim, allowing XML node injection. The PoC was executed against @xmldom/xmldom@0.9.9 and confirmed that PI data containing '?>' breaks the PI serialization boundary, injecting an attacker-controlled ProcessingInstruction into the re-parsed document (Test 1 PASSED). Comment injection was also confirmed (Test 2 PASSED). The serialized output '<?xml-stylesheet href="style.css"?><?inject EVOHUNT_PI_INJECTION_CONFIRMED ?>' demonstrates the advisory-described security consequence: arbitrary XML nodes injected through unvalidated PI serialization.
evidence: Test 1 PASSED: PI data 'href="style.css"?><?inject EVOHUNT_PI_INJECTION_CONFIRMED ?>' breaks the PI boundary in serialized output '<?xml-stylesheet href="style.css"?><?inject EVOHUNT_PI_INJECTION_CONFIRMED ?>?>', confirming the advisory-described missing '?>' validation in createProcessingInstruction.
Test 2 PASSED: Comment data 'original--><EVOHUNT_COMMENT_INJECTION_CONFIRMED/><!--restored' breaks the comment boundary, injecting an element in the re-parsed DOM.
The vulnerable serializer code at lib/dom.js:3064 emits PI data verbatim: buf.push('<?', node.target, ' ', node.data, '?>') with no escaping of '?>' within data.
The vulnerable createProcessingInstruction at lib/dom.js:2266 stores data without checking for '?>' sequences.

poc_executions.observed_effect: PI injection confirmed: serialized output '<?xml-stylesheet href="style.css"?><?inject EVOHUNT_PI_INJECTION_CONFIRMED ?>?>' shows PI boundary broken by '?>' in data, injecting a second PI confirmed by re-parsing. Comment injection confirmed: serialized output '<root><!--original--><EVOHUNT_COMMENT_INJECTION_CONFIRMED/><!--restored--></root>' shows comment boundary broken by '-->', injecting an element confirmed in re-parsed DOM. Test 3 (requireWellFormed) crashed due to API incompatibility but does not affect core vulnerability confirmation.
